_doc: Canonical card catalogue for the CDiscourse UX/UI Roadmap GitHub Project (#1 under kyleruff1). Only contains the QOL-NNN cards that are NOT already covered by an existing TL/VG/BR/SC/ST/EV/SW/IX/PR/HOST/GAL/RULE/AN/PM issue. Source of truth for syncUxProjectBoard.js (dry-run) and applyUxProjectBoard.sh (operator-runnable). Do not put any secret values here. Body fields render verbatim into GitHub issue bodies. Field options are constrained to the live project schema captured 2026-05-18: Priority P0/P1/P2 (no P3), Effort S/M/L/XL (no XS), Release 6.5/6.6/6.7/6.8 (no 6.9), Epic from the existing 14, Phase Backlog/Design/Build/Review/Done/Blocked. Status defaults to Todo on creation.

supersededByExisting.QOL-001: #1 TL-001 Make Timeline the default room landing mode
supersededByExisting.QOL-003: #9 SC-001 Consolidate controls into the side action rail
supersededByExisting.QOL-011: #25 PR-003 Avatar upload policy and storage
supersededByExisting.QOL-012: #27 HOST-001 Dev hosting architecture for cdiscourse.com/dev
supersededByExisting.QOL-013: #28 HOST-002 Dev environment banner and safety boundary
supersededByExisting.QOL-014: #29 HOST-003 Deployment smoke checklist + #35 AN-002 Visual QA snapshots

cards.title: QOL-015 - Admin email delivery validation (mock first, live operator-gated)
body: ## Goal
Validate the `request-argument-deletion` admin notification path end-to-end before public dev hosting goes live.
## Acceptance
- Local/mock test path proves the Edge Function builds the admin notification payload correctly without sending a real email.
- Resend `not_configured` fallback is exercised in tests and returns a non-secret response.
- Test asserts Authorization header, RESEND key, and admin email addresses are never echoed back to the client.
- Dry-run path documented in `docs/admin-email-validation-plan.md`.
- Live email send remains operator-gated; this card does NOT authorise a live send.
## Out of scope
- Sending real emails to real admin inboxes (deferred to QOL-016 operator step).
- Adding new admin notification surfaces.
## Non-negotiables
- No service-role usage from client code.
- No raw admin email addresses printed in test fixtures, snapshots, or commit messages.

cards.title: QOL-016 - Supabase Auth email + redirect settings audit for cdiscourse.com/dev
body: ## Goal
Audit Supabase Auth email settings (signup confirmation, password reset, magic link, email change, invite) against the cdiscourse.com/dev hosting target before public dev deployment.
## Acceptance
- Checklist enumerates every Auth email template, its sender, its subject, its redirect URL.
- All redirect URLs include cdiscourse.com/dev (or whichever URL HOST-001 lands on) and exclude localhost from production allow-list.
- Local docs note required Supabase project settings (Auth -> URL Configuration, Auth -> Email Templates).
- No live email send by this card.
- No secret values written to docs.
## Depends on
- HOST-001 chooses final URL shape (cdiscourse.com/dev vs dev.cdiscourse.com).
- QOL-015 admin notification path proven on the mock side first.

cards.title: QOL-017 - GitHub Projects automation script (sync roadmap to Project #1)
body: ## Goal

Keep `docs/ux-ui-project-board.md`, repo issues, and Project #1 in sync via a small, safe, dry-run-default script. Avoid hand-editing 35+ project items in the GitHub UI when a doc/issue rename or rebucket happens.
## Acceptance
- `scripts/github/syncUxProjectBoard.js` exists, defaults to `--dry-run`, only mutates GitHub when `--apply` is passed AND `gh` is installed AND `gh auth status` is clean with `project` scope.
- `scripts/github/uxBoardCards.json` is the single source-of-truth catalogue with field options validated against the live project schema.
- `scripts/github/applyUxProjectBoard.sh` is the operator-runnable equivalent (Bash-only, no Node deps) for environments where Node isn't available.
- npm scripts `github:ux-board:dry` and `github:ux-board:apply` wired up.
- Tests cover catalogue validation, prefix dedupe, secret-shape rejection, and dry-run-no-network.
- Script never prints `gh` tokens, Bearer tokens, or any `Authorization:` header value.
## Status

Initial scaffold landed in this commit. Future iteration: add `--apply` path that actually issues `gh issue create / project item-add / project item-edit` calls deterministically, not just delegates to the bash script.

cards.title: QOL-018 - Repo-local Claude agent charters for CDiscourse
body: ## Goal

Define repo-local charters for the recurring agent roles future Claude Code sessions take on (project-board-manager, ux-board-designer, timeline-gameboard-implementer, sidecar-tools-implementer, supabase-email-validation-tester, bot-corpus-runner, qa-secret-scan-verifier).
## Acceptance
- `docs/agent-charters.md` enumerates each role with mission, allowed tools, forbidden actions, files to read first, verification commands, and final-report format.
- Charters are documentation only â€” they do NOT create `.claude/agents/*.md` files unless the repo establishes that convention later.
- Bot skill SKILL.md files under `.claude/skills/bot-provocateur/` and `.claude/skills/bot-revocateur/` are unchanged (validator hashes must match).
- `npm run skills:validate` continues to pass.
## Why not .claude/agents

- Charters are role guidance, not executable subagent definitions. The skills convention (`disable-model-invocation: true`, manual operator-only) intentionally rejects model-spawned bot behaviour. Charters preserve that boundary while still giving future sessions clear scope.

cards.title: QOL-019 - Bot tester prompt refresh: more dynamic, still safe
body: ## Goal

Make bot-fixture prompts (open-room engagement + existing-room engagement + xAI adversarial thread continuation) more dynamic and lifelike while preserving the existing safety contract (no persona pretends to be a real human, no canned phrases, structured quote + axis + mechanism + evidenceDebt pressure).
## Acceptance
- Prompt variants pull from a wider lexicon without introducing forbidden tokens (`troll`, `bot`, `astroturfer`, etc. â€” see anti-amplification doctrine).
- Anti-axis-repeat doctrine continues to rotate disagreement axes inside a single room.
- Heat-band escalation copy varies but never claims correctness/truth/winner.
- Some dev/test rooms reach HOT band naturally inside `bot:engage:open:tiny` and `bot:engage:existing:pilot`.
- No live run by this card; tests + dry-run only.
- Operator approval still required for any live `--pilot` invocation.
## Non-negotiables
- No raw hostile text from xAI source-harvest input is rendered verbatim into a generated move.
- No new external AI provider added.

cards.title: QOL-020 - Open-room engagement runner patch: filter, side-respect, coverage targeting
body: ## Goal
Tighten `scripts/bot-fixtures/runOpenRoomEngagementBots.js` and `scripts/bot-fixtures/engageExistingRooms.js` against three classes of issue surfaced by the 2026-05-18 dry/live runs.
## Acceptance
- Rooms with `moveCount < 1` are skipped (no bot enters an empty room with no opening claim).
- When a bot user is already a participant on a side, the runner respects that existing side and does not re-enrol on the opposite side.
- Coverage targeting hits 33-66% of eligible open rooms per run, deterministic given the same seed.

- `--dry` mode still emits the full event set (`run_start`, `room_candidate`, `bot_assignment`, `move_prompt_built`, `move_rendered`, `move_validated`, `submit_attempt`, `submit_result`, `room_heat_update`, `room_summary`, `run_summary`).
- No service-role usage. No direct insert into `public.arguments`. Posts go only through `submit-argument`.
- Operator approval gate intact for `--pilot`.

cards.title: QOL-021 - UX storyboard canon and storyline-narrative-officer skill
body: ## Goal

Establish a durable UX storyboard canon under `docs/ux-storyboards/` plus a manual dev/design-only skill that keeps it coherent, so design, product, issue, and implementation work all reference the same intended experience.
## Acceptance

- `docs/ux-storyboards/` holds: README, two scenario storyboards (roommates/dishes public, band/rent private), interaction taxonomy, missing-capabilities report, terminology rules, storyboard-to-roadmap map, generated terminology audit.
- `.claude/skills/storyline-narrative-officer/SKILL.md` exists with valid manual-only frontmatter (disable-model-invocation true, user-invocable true, invocation user, effort high).

- The skill states it is dev/design-only with no production use, makes no AI/Supabase/email calls, enforces 'do not call it a game in UI' and 'prefer Argument over Debate in UI', and requires issue-deduping before proposing cards.
- A test validates the skill frontmatter and required content.
## Status
Delivered by the storyboard pass. This card tracks the canon for future maintenance.

cards.title: QOL-022 - User-facing terminology scrub: no game, prefer Argument over Debate
body: ## Goal
Scrub prohibited and discouraged terminology from normal-user UI copy. CDiscourse is an argument product: user-facing copy must not say 'game' and should not say 'debate'.
## Acceptance
- `npm run ux:terminology:audit` reports zero live prohibited violations and the discouraged count trends to zero.

- User-facing 'Debate' / 'debate room' / 'moderator' copy in mounted screens (CreateDebateForm, JoinDebatePanel, DebateDetailHeader, admin tabs, constitution copy) is reworded per `docs/ux-storyboards/terminology-and-copy-rules.md`.
- `npm run ux:terminology:audit --strict` exits 0.
- Copy tests assert the reworded labels.
## Out of scope
- Renaming the `debates` database table or internal identifiers (`gameCopy`, `argumentGameSurface`).
## Non-negotiables
- No database table rename. No migration. Internal code identifiers untouched.

cards.title: QOL-023 - Payment and screenshot evidence metadata object
body: ## Goal
Extend the EV-001 evidence object so a payment screenshot can be attached with structured metadata, per the band-space rent storyboard.
## Acceptance
- The evidence object gains optional, additive payment fields: amount, date, redacted payer/payee, note text, claimed applicability. No breaking change to the existing EvidenceArtifact.
- Payer/payee and account data are stored redacted; raw financial account data is never required or stored.
- Evidence confidence is always user-asserted, never system-proven.
- A payment evidence object never produces a truth value or a point-standing delta on its own.
## Depends on
- EV-001 (evidence object model v1).
## Non-negotiables
- No raw financial account numbers. No truth verdict from evidence existence.

cards.title: QOL-024 - Evidence applicability dispute flow
body: ## Goal
Add the evidence applicability axis: a structured way to dispute what a piece of evidence applies to (e.g. which month a payment covers), separate from disputing that it exists.
## Acceptance
- An evidence object carries an applicability status distinct from its existence and source-chain status.
- Structured evidence responses exist: Accept, Accept with caveat, Dispute date, Dispute amount, Dispute applicability, Request source, Request clarification.
- Any non-Accept response requires a clarification body.
- Disputing applicability changes a status, never declares the evidence false; copy uses 'Applicability disputed', never proof/true/false.
## Depends on
## Non-negotiables
- Evidence is never treated as truth. No winner/loser copy.

cards.title: QOL-025 - Invite to signup or auth to argument-room return path
body: ## Goal
Build the invite-by-email backend and the auth deep link so an invited respondent lands directly in the specific argument room, fast â€” these are real-time conflicts being de-escalated.
## Acceptance
- Inviting by email creates an invite record (see `docs/invite-flow.md`); RLS lets the inviter manage it and the invitee see their own.
- An invited new user completes signup and lands on the invited room.
- An invited existing user completes auth and lands on the invited room.
- The invited respondent enters as a primary participant with no observer/side modal (consistent with Stage 6.4 seamless entry).
- Invite email subject/body never say 'debate challenge' or 'game invite'.
## Non-negotiables
- No service-role in client. No broad user search exposed. No emails sent until operator-gated.

cards.title: QOL-026 - Public and private room visibility transition rules
body: ## Goal
Define and enforce room visibility: create a room private, or transition a public room to private, with the correct RLS and the correct, neutral consequences.
## Acceptance
- A room can be created private and never appears on any public list.
- The room creator can transition public to private; non-participant read access is revoked by RLS.
- On transition, rejected chime-in branches are retained (muted), never deleted.
- The transition triggers neutral observer notifications (see QOL-027) and never shaming copy.
## Depends on
- GAME-005 (public seats) for the chime-in interaction; QOL-027 for the notifications.
## Non-negotiables
- RLS is never disabled. No content is hard-deleted on transition.

cards.title: QOL-027 - Invite and response notification lifecycle
body: ## Goal

Build the in-app notification lifecycle for an argument: invite, new response, concession challenged, source requested, evidence supplied, chime-in posted, room made private, chime-in rejected, argument settled.
## Acceptance
- Each storyboard trigger produces a notification with neutral copy.
- Notification copy never exposes data the room visibility forbids.
- Opening a notification routes to the room with the relevant node active.
- The chime-in-rejected notification is neutral and never shames the observer.
## Out of scope
- Push notifications (excluded by v1 scope). In-app notifications only.
## Taxonomy note
- No NOTIF epic exists; this card sits under Interaction. A dedicated epic is only worth creating if notification scope grows well beyond this card.

cards.title: QOL-028 - Concession list and acceptance gradient
body: ## Goal
Add the itemized concession list to the response composer and the per-concession acceptance gradient the receiver uses to respond to each conceded point.
## Acceptance
- A response can carry multiple separately-addressable concession items.
- Each received concession exposes a 5-level acceptance gradient: Agree, Agree with caveat, Disagree based on framing, Disagree based on context, Disagree based on fact.
- Any gradient choice other than Agree requires a clarification body.
- The chosen gradient sets the room active-disagreement classification (framing/context/fact) and shows on the node.
- A fist-bump / acknowledgment reaction exists as a low-friction partial-resolution affordance carrying no score and no verdict.
## Depends on
- The point-standing economy (ConcessionEffect) and RULE-004 (advisory review).
## Non-negotiables
- A concession is a scoring repair, never a defeat. No winner/loser copy.

cards.title: QOL-029 - Linked prior argument reference as context
body: ## Goal
Let a new argument room reference an earlier settled room as context, with an access check that respects the prior room visibility.
## Acceptance
- A new room can reference a prior room by ID; the prior room title shows as a context chip.
- A locked prior room is never re-opened or mutated by the link.
- A private prior room content opens only for users authorized on that original room; an unauthorized user sees the title only.
- Relevant resolved tangent nodes from the prior room can surface as context.
## Non-negotiables
- The access check is enforced by RLS, not only by UI. A locked room stays immutable.
